briefing.headline: Rig up a Swim Jig — finesse post-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Swim Jig is a strong choice for post-spawn with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: On lighter jigheads (1/8-3/16 oz) for Mooch Minnow swimbaits, cut off the tail tabs so the bait rolls more on the lighter head — gives more action for fish higher in the water column. On heavier heads (3/8-1/2 oz) keep the tail tabs intact as they won't affect action with the heavier weight

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better act
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'For skip casting, use the heaviest line you can get away with — heavier line makes line management easier. 50lb braid for frogs and swim jigs in colored water. 20lb fluorocarbon for sparser cover or clearer water. 14lb fluoro for finesse jig...
Hackney: 'Uses braid even in clear water when swimming jigs. As long as it is a moving bait, line type doesn't seem to matter — bass chomp regardless.'

Wheeler: The Punch Out Craw has five manifestations - full glide bait on 4/0 hook and 1/2-oz sinker, separated claws for football jig or Biffle Hardhead, trimmed small craw for swim jig/flipping jig/chatterbait/shakyhead, shortened torso for even ...
Yamamoto pro Hite: Standard Zako for ChatterBaits (subtle natural action), Kickin' Zako (paddle tail) for deep heavy ChatterBaits on ledges with poor light penetration and for swim jigs exclusively

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal post-spawn fish hunker down at 6-12ft near cover. Males may temporarily leave fry. Focus on the deeper side of transition areas — channel edges, deeper points, and offshore structure near spawning flats.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
